splash.body: Third party media plans! Scary? I think not! Here's all it is: campaign specialists take the data from Slingshot. Then, they format it for the client’s agency spec sheet. Poof! All done!
Let's learn about third party media plans. Click each step of the workflow to uncover more details.

conclusion.body: Nice job! Media planning isn't too bad, when you know who handles what. In the next few lessons, let's see how to build different types of media plans, as well as how to build a pitch deck.

items.title: Step 1: Create Media Plan and Pull Avails in Slingshot
items.body: Campaign specialists plan out line items in Slingshot and pull the avails. That's how you figure out the percentage of inventory that's still available. Work with your pod to recommend revisions. Afterwards campaign specialists make the appropriate revisions to the media plan.

items.title: Step 2: Revise Media Plan and Re-Pull Avails in Slingshot
items.body: Some line items in your plan might not have enough impression inventory for your budget. It happens! Revise these line items and re-pull the impressions within Slingshot. You might need to revise your media plan a few times.

items.title: Step 3: Make the Third Party Media Plan
items.body: First, campaign specialists export a Pandora version of the media plan using Slingshot. Then, they copy the information to fit in the client-provided agency spec sheet. They'll need to check the ad placements, impressions, rates and other data for accuracy. This process is more or less the same as building an order in a third party media buying system like Prisma. Campaign specialists log on to Prisma and build the plan in Prisma’s interface. Afterwards, the account strategist or account executive sends it to the client.

items.title: Step 4: Revise Third Party Media Plan
items.body: Clients might have feedback, and that leads to revisions! This means building pulling more avails, and rebuilding the media plan by campaign specialists. Afterwards, the account strategist or account executive will present the plan back to the client.

items.title: Step 5: Recheck Avails after getting all Insertion Order signatures
items.body: After everyone's signed the insertion order, campaign specialists recheck impression inventory. This is to make sure that the inventory is still available to book in Slingshot.
